T1_fish_name_vs_commodity.verdict: fish behaves like a countable noun (stem-side); the specific numeral+fish = star-name prediction is NOT clearly supported: most compounds sit bare mid-text, more commodity-like than named-entity-like

T2_jar_lr_diversity.verdict: jar bolts onto many stems, is rarely followed -> suffix fingerprint; fish shows content-word fingerprint

T3_terminal_concentration.verdict: a small near-closed terminal set (33 signs ever final); jar+lance co-occur in only 1/111 -> near-complementary, suffix-like

T4_name_formula.verdict: 55 distinct stems feed one jar suffix across 98 positions -> many-names-one-ending

jar_man.note: 2000 per-text shuffles; p = P(random order reaches observed one-directional consistency). Corrects the chat's reversed man->jar direction: the fixed pair is jar THEN man.

T6_number_sensitivity.verdict: jar is number-independent -> case/honorific, not a plural marker

T7_modifier_head.verdict: NULL: numerals precede and follow content signs about equally; clean modifier-head order not present in this corpus
